prompt: Create a cinematic travel-poster style photograph of Lisbon: a narrow, steep old European street descending between tall aged apartment buildings, with cobblestone pavement, twin tram rails, weathered cream and yellow facades, many rectangular windows, overhead electrical wires, wall graffiti on both sides, and warm golden-hour sunlight. Center a classic vintage yellow Lisbon tram coming uphill toward the viewer, framed symmetrically in the middle of the street, with a glimpse of distant water and hazy horizon at the far end. Add a clean editorial typography overlay across the center: exactly 2 text elements, a large bold distressed white all-caps title reading “LISBON” and a thin white handwritten cursive subtitle beneath reading “Lost in the right direction”. Include exactly 3 small decorative graphic elements around the subtitle: one thin horizontal white line extending left, one thin horizontal white line extending right, and one small white outlined diamond centered below the subtitle. Use a warm moody color grade, high contrast, realistic detail, slight film grain, deep perspective lines, no people, no extra logos, no watermark.
